texto: Like billowing clouds, Like the incessant gurgle of the brook,The longing of the spirit can never be stilled.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Hildegard of Bingen: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “Like billowing clouds, Like the incessant gurgle of the brook,The longing of the spirit can never…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que fe, endlessness, longing pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e longing.

texto: Fhe Creator and Lord of all so loved the world that He sent His Son for its salvation the Prince and Savior of the faithful who washed and dried our wounds and from Him also came that most sweet medicine from which all the good things of salvation flow.
explicacao: Citação de Hildegard of Bingen sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “Fhe Creator and Lord of all so loved the world that He sent His Son for its salvation the Prince …” articula fe, god, reflexao em registro de fe, com relevância para contextos como reflexao.

seo.descriptionSeo: Citação de Hildegard of Bingen sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “Fhe Creator and Lord of all so loved the world that He sent His Son for its salvation…

frase_original: Fhe Creator and Lord of all so loved the world that He sent His Son for its salvation the Prince and Savior of the faithful who washed and dried our wounds and from Him also came that most sweet medicine from which all the good things of salvation flow.

texto: Beryl: Beryl is a warm gemstone which develops, between the third hour and midday, from the foam of water when the sun burns it severely. Its power is thus more from air and water than from fire, but nevertheless it has some of the properties of fire. And if a man has drunk or eaten poison, then he should place a little beryl in spring water and drink it at once. Continue for five days drinking it once a day while fasting, and the poison will foam up through vomiting, or it will pass out of him through the rear.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Hildegard of Bingen sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. A afirmação “Beryl: Beryl is a warm gemstone which develops, between the third hour and midday, from the foam …” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, mysticism, beryl, útil em momentos de reflexao.

And if a man has drunk or eaten poison, then he should place a little beryl in spring water and drink it at once.
